input.title: Fleet Management System
input.description: Fleet Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, InfluxDB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Vehicle Registration
requirements: Users must be able to complete vehicle registration through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing vehicle registration to continue.
Vehicle Registration must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for vehicle registration should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Driver Registration and Licensing
requirements: Users must be able to complete driver registration and licensing through the approved entry points provided by the platform.

Driver Registration and Licensing must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for driver registration and licensing should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-03.title: Vehicle Assignment to Driver
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete vehicle assignment to driver according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving vehicle assignment to driver changes.
Vehicle Assignment to Driver must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for vehicle assignment to driver.

FR-04.title: GPS Real-time Tracking
requirements: The system must capture gps real-time tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
GPS Real-time Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile gps real-time tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during gps real-time tracking.

FR-05.title: Route Planning and Optimization
requirements: The system must support route planning and optimization as a complete business workflow within the application.
Route Planning and Optimization should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with route planning and optimization from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to route planning and optimization for operational support.

FR-06.title: Trip Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end trip management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Trip Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to trip management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within trip management and retain operational history for reporting.
